destinations.evidence: sync_from_storage reads count, each agent address, and liveness; errors include index/agent context; it replaces agents, sets last_synced_block, and marks hydrated.
Heartbeat storage helpers expose the count, indexed agent address, and liveness-critical fields used by hydration.
destinations.snippet:
pub fn read_heartbeat_agent_count<DB: Database>(...) -> Result<u64, String>
pub fn read_heartbeat_agent_at<DB: Database>(...) -> Result<Address, String>
pub fn read_heartbeat_agent_liveness<DB: Database>(...) -> Result<HeartbeatAgentLiveness, String>

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port: heartbeat hydration replaces memory from on-chain liveness state and fails on read errors.
rationale: The later heartbeat candidate refines the earlier field-preservation invariant with full replacement and error handling semantics.
statement: HeartbeatTracker storage hydration must read the on-chain agent count, each agent address, and each agent liveness record, replace the in-memory map with exact hydrated records, set last_synced_block and hydrated=true, and return an error on any agent read failure.
suggested_falsification_tests: Mock two agents in heartbeat storage; assert sync_from_storage replaces stale tracker entries and sets hydrated/last_synced_block.
Make one agent read fail and assert sync_from_storage returns Err without silently partial-hydrating.
summary: Heartbeat hydration mirrors chain liveness storage.
test_or_falsification_candidate: HeartbeatTracker::sync_from_storage unit test for replacement and error propagation.
title: Heartbeat hydration mirrors on-chain liveness storage
